title: rm: add validation to args and all option
body:
When arg is greater than one and the all option is used, error need to be output.

Signed-off-by: Zhou Hao <zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com>

comments.body: Why.  If a user specifies an image to delete and accidently adds a `-a`; then the tool would delete all containers, instead of giving an error.  Why would you want the command to continue and potentially do something the user did not intend?

>Why. If a user specifies an image do delete ...

It seems my idea is wrong. 
Updated. Added similar verification in the `rm` function.
Change LGTM, it would be nice to have a test case.  Build issues are not related, hopefully they will be cleaned up with another round of testing.
